positiveAcceptance: An active canonical Review session is exported through the native Review menu, main-owned Save As dialog, canonical V1 serializer, and atomic external write.
The exported canonical Review Packet V1 is selected again through the native Review menu and main-owned local picker.
Import opens the existing Review surface without mutating the manuscript.
Main rebuilds one ready exact-text plan from the active project, current saved document, and imported change identifier.
The visible Apply button sends only requestId and changeId through the production command bridge.
Apply changes the canonical scene through the existing safe writer, reloads the editor, exposes a receipt, and leaves a readable recovery snapshot containing the pre-apply text.
The Electron acceptance run observes zero HTTP, HTTPS, WS, or WSS requests.
negativeAcceptance: Wrong project blocks with zero enabled Apply actions and zero disk mutation.
Wrong scene blocks with zero enabled Apply actions and zero disk mutation.
Stale baseline blocks with zero enabled Apply actions and zero disk mutation.
No match blocks with zero enabled Apply actions and zero disk mutation.
Duplicate match blocks with zero enabled Apply actions and zero disk mutation.
Malformed JSON does not activate a Review session and does not mutate disk.
Closed Review session rejects Apply and does not mutate disk.
Dirty editor blocks Apply while preserving unsaved editor text and canonical disk text.
A delayed plan refresh from an older imported session cannot overwrite a newer active session.

authorityBoundary.reviewSurfaceTruth: in-memory preview and receipt visibility only

auditFindingsResolved.finding: The checked-in native menu artifact was stale and omitted delivered Review commands.
auditFindingsResolved.resolution: The canonical menu snapshot, normalized runtime artifact, and artifact lock were regenerated from the current menu source.

auditFindingsResolved.finding: Menu items without actionArg sent an own undefined field into strict command payload validation.
auditFindingsResolved.resolution: Configured menu payload construction now omits actionArg unless a non-empty string exists.

auditFindingsResolved.finding: Concurrent asynchronous plan refreshes could attach an older session plan to a newer session.
auditFindingsResolved.resolution: Plan attachment now requires the active sessionId and sourcePacketHash to match the captured refresh token.

auditFindingsResolved.finding: The default editable roman section kind was excluded from otherwise bounded current-document exact apply.
auditFindingsResolved.resolution: Single and same-file batch exact apply accept scene, chapter-file, and roman-section while materials, reference, mindmap, print, external, and cross-scene targets remain blocked.

auditFindingsResolved.finding: Two Sector M DOCX toolbar assertions still expected a pre-confirmation command shape and exact whitespace from an older mainline.
auditFindingsResolved.resolution: The test-only assertions now verify the current confirmed intent payload and semantic runtime command call without changing DOCX production behavior.

machineChecks.command: node --test canonical packet, review mutate port, and Review surface UI targeted set
node --test active Review Bridge, hostile DOCX, menu, command bus, preload, and Review UI regression set

machineChecks.notes: Package manifests are unchanged; production dependency audit is clean.

machineChecks.command: node --test broad Review Bridge, menu, and command bus audit set
node --test exact planner, exact safe writer, DOCX review local-file, preview-session, and preflight post-commit set

reasons: Five historical contour allowlist tests inspect the current branch diff against their own older binding base and reject every later contour by design.
The superseded P0 safety-kernel test still asserts that Review Bridge has no runtime wiring, which contradicts later delivered contours and current mainline truth.

nonClaims: No DOCX tracked-change apply is claimed.
No cross-scene or multi-file transaction is claimed.
No Word or Google synchronization is claimed.
No cloud, account, authorization, or network integration is introduced.
Crash reconciliation is not claimed by this contour and remains Phase 02.
Release readiness is not claimed before delivery, crash reconciliation, file authority hardening, Markdown completion, and bounded golden acceptance.
nextStep: finish policy checks, commit, push, open PR, merge after green checks, then immediately rebind delivery truth before Phase 02
